format: Use a clean, professional font like Arial, Calibri, or Times New Roman in 10-12pt size.
Keep consistent formatting throughout your resume with proper alignment and spacing.
Save your resume as a PDF unless the employer specifically requests another format.
Use white space effectively - don't cram too much information onto one page.
content: Start with a strong summary or objective statement that highlights your key qualifications.
Use bullet points instead of paragraphs to make your achievements easy to scan.
Include keywords from the job description to pass applicant tracking systems (ATS).
Proofread multiple times or use tools like Grammarly to catch errors.

before: Research the company's mission, values, recent news, and key executives.
Prepare 5-10 thoughtful questions to ask the interviewer about the role and company.
Practice common interview questions with a friend or in front of a mirror.
Plan your outfit the night before and test your technology for virtual interviews.
during: Arrive 10-15 minutes early for in-person interviews or log in 5 minutes early for virtual ones.
Maintain good eye contact and positive body language throughout the interview.
Use the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) for behavioral questions.
Be honest about your experience and focus on transferable skills.
after: Send a personalized thank-you email within 24 hours of the interview.
Reference specific points from your conversation to show you were listening.
Connect with the interviewer on LinkedIn with a personalized message.
Follow up if you don't hear back within the timeline they provided.

technical: Identify in-demand skills in your industry through job postings and industry reports.
Use online platforms like Coursera, Udemy, or edX for structured learning.
Build projects to apply and demonstrate your new technical skills.
Contribute to open-source projects to gain real-world experience.
soft: Practice active listening in conversations to improve communication skills.
Join Toastmasters or similar groups to enhance public speaking abilities.
Take on leadership roles in volunteer organizations or community projects.
Develop emotional intelligence through mindfulness and self-reflection practices.

entry_level: Apply for internships or entry-level positions to gain initial experience.
Network with alumni and professionals in your desired field.
Consider graduate assistantships or fellowships for advanced positions.
Be open to starting in related roles to get your foot in the door.
mid_career: Seek mentorship from senior professionals in your industry.
Consider professional certifications to advance your expertise.
Develop leadership and management skills for career progression.
Build a personal brand through speaking engagements or writing.
career_change: Research transferable skills that apply to your new desired field.
Consider informational interviews to learn about different industries.
Start with side projects or freelance work to test your new career interest.
Be prepared for a potential temporary salary decrease when changing fields.

strategy: Set daily or weekly goals for applications, networking, and skill development.
Use multiple job search platforms: LinkedIn, Indeed, company career pages, and industry-specific sites.
Create a tracking system to manage your applications and follow-ups.
Focus on quality over quantity - tailor each application rather than mass applying.
networking: Attend industry conferences, meetups, and virtual events to expand your network.
Request informational interviews to learn about companies and roles.
Join professional associations and online communities in your field.
Maintain relationships with your network even when you're not actively job searching.

content: Showcase 5-10 of your best projects rather than everything you've done.
Include case studies that explain your process, challenges, and solutions.
Add testimonials from clients, colleagues, or supervisors to build credibility.
Regularly update your portfolio with new projects and improved work.
presentation: Ensure your portfolio is mobile-friendly and loads quickly.
Use high-quality images and videos to showcase your work effectively.
Include clear descriptions of your role and contributions to each project.
Add contact information and links to your professional social media profiles.

linkedin: Use a professional headshot and customize your LinkedIn URL.
Write a compelling headline that summarizes your expertise and value proposition.
Request recommendations from colleagues, supervisors, and clients.
Share relevant content and engage with others' posts to increase visibility.
personal_website: Create a clean, professional website that showcases your work and expertise.
Include an about page that tells your professional story.
Add a blog to share your insights and establish thought leadership.
Ensure your website is optimized for search engines with relevant keywords.
